items.title: Sweetgreen Pares 26% Drop as Parasite Tied to Taco Bell Lettuce
items.description: Sweetgreen Inc. shares surged on Friday, halting a four-day skid, after US regulators said a parasite outbreak that’s sickened thousands across several states was traced to shredded iceberg lettuce se

items.title: Griffin Wants to Buy an Old Cottage Across From New Headquarters
items.description: Ken Griffin has gone to extraordinary lengths to assemble parcels of land around his planned Citadel headquarters in Miami, including buying up all 138 units in a condo building across the street.

items.title: US Gasoline Prices Are Heading to $4 a Gallon Again
items.description: It’s the middle of summer driving season, when Americans typically embark on road trips and burn through millions of barrels of gasoline each day. They’ll be paying more for every fill up.

items.title: How New PM Andy Burnham Affects Your Money and UK Markets: Live Q&A
items.description: Bloomberg reporters answer your questions about the UK’s new prime minister Andy Burnham and what his policies mean for your money and markets in a Live Q&A on Tuesday, July 16 at 2 p.m. BST

items.title: Foundever Strikes Deal With Creditors to Cut Debt Load
items.description: Troubled business services giant Foundever Group has reached an agreement with a group of creditors to reduce its multi-billion dollar debt load, with the company’s controlling shareholder injecting f

items.title: Wild Weather Disrupts Copper Mines and Ports in Central Chile
items.description: Copper mines and ports in central Chile curtailed some operations in the face of a powerful winter storm, while major mining installations in the north remained unaffected.

items.title: Hedge Fund Blasts Settlement With SEC After ‘Gag Rule’ Lifted
items.description: Connecticut hedge fund Deccan Value Investors is speaking out about its 2022 settlement of Securities and Exchange Commission claims that it breached its fiduciary duty to two university endowments af

items.title: Dollar Hedging Costs Sink to Their Lowest Level This Year
items.description: The cost of hedging against swings in the dollar has fallen to its lowest level this year, signaling that traders see little chance of a major catalyst disrupting the world’s reserve currency despite

items.title: Anglo Selects Penny’s Group as Preferred Bidder for De Beers
items.description: Anglo American Plc has selected a group led by De Beers’ former boss Gareth Penny as the preferred bidder for its sale of the world’s biggest diamond mining company, people with knowledge of the matte

items.title: BASF Seeks Banks to Lead IPO of €20 Billion Agrichemical Unit
items.description: BASF SE has invited banks to pitch for roles on an initial public offering of its agrichemical division in Frankfurt, in what could be one of the largest offerings the bourse has seen in recent years.

items.title: Chips Stocks Sink Into Bear Market as 105% AI Rally Fizzles
items.description: A closely-watched index of semiconductor stocks is set to fall into a bear market, unwinding a chunk of the blistering memory-led rally that saw the gauge more than double in just three months.

items.title: Neglected Quant Safety Trade Makes a Comeback as AI Risks Rise
items.description: As worries build around the artificial intelligence trade, investors are starting to seek shelter in a more pedestrian corner of the stock market: companies with sturdy finances.

items.title: Hyperscalers Drag Down Bond Gauges Across Global Markets
items.description: The bonds sold by hyperscalers to fuel their artificial intelligence ambitions have become a drag on investor portfolios from London to Tokyo.

items.title: IBM’s Warning Is a ‘Hammer’ Slamming Down on Tech’s AI Outsiders
items.description: International Business Machines Corp.’s warning this week that its sales are falling well short of expectations highlights a growing divide between artificial intelligence winners and everyone else in

items.title: US Stocks Pare Losses as Investors Evaluate Kimi K3 AI Model
items.description: US stocks fell on Friday as semiconductor names dipped following the reveal of an artificial-intelligence model from a Chinese startup.

items.title: Crop Markets Mixed on Shipping Worries, US-China Trade Concerns
items.description: Crop markets were mixed Friday amid lingering tensions in the Black Sea and Strait of Hormuz, as well as concerns over US trade ties with China.

items.title: Gold Trims Weekly Drop on Dip-Buying Despite Rate Hike Odds
items.description: Gold trimmed its weekly loss as some investors bought on dips after the precious metal broke below a key price level, even as escalating war tensions kept interest rate hike bets alive.

items.title: Chip Stocks Poised for Bear Market in AI Unwind: Markets Wrap
items.description: A selloff in chipmakers gathered pace on Friday, driving the group that has led this year’s stock rally toward a bear market on worries that the artificial-intelligence spending spree is becoming hard

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
items.description: Oil surged on growing fears the US and Iran could return to full war, threatening flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: CoreLogic Tweaks Terms on Struggling Debt Sale, Boosts Loan
items.description: CoreLogic Inc. amended the terms of its roughly $5.3 billion debt offering and shifted part of the funding to loans from bonds after struggling with lukewarm investor demand.
